name: AlertDialog
description: A modal dialog that requires explicit user acknowledgment. Used for destructive or irreversible actions. Built on Base UI AlertDialog with StyleX styling.

Root.description: State container. Renders no DOM element.

defaultOpen.description: Initial open state for uncontrolled mode.

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the dialog opens or closes.

onOpenChangeComplete.description: Callback fired after open/close animations complete.

Trigger.description: Button that opens the dialog. Unstyled by default — use the render prop to swap in a styled Button.

render.description: Replace the underlying element. Use to render a styled Button.

dataAttributes.data-popup-open: Present when the dialog is open.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the trigger is disabled.

Portal.description: Renders children in a React portal outside the DOM tree.

keepMounted.description: Keep in DOM when closed. Defaults to true for exit animations.

container.description: Target parent element for the portal.

Backdrop.description: Semi-transparent overlay behind the dialog. Has fade animation via global CSS.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the dialog is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the dialog is closed.

Popup.description: Centered card containing the dialog content. Viewport is wrapped internally for centering and scroll management.

initialFocus.description: Element to focus when the dialog opens.

finalFocus.description: Element to focus when the dialog closes.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the dialog is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the dialog is closed.
dataAttributes.data-nested: Present when nested inside another dialog.
dataAttributes.data-nested-dialog-open: Present when a child dialog is open.

Title.description: Dialog heading. Automatically wired to aria-labelledby.

Description.description: Dialog body text. Automatically wired to aria-describedby.

Actions.description: Flex row for action buttons (Cancel/Confirm). Custom part not in Base UI.

Close.description: Button that closes the dialog on click. Used for both Cancel and Confirm actions. Unstyled — use the render prop to swap in a styled Button.

render.description: Replace the underlying element. Use to render a styled Button.

cssRequirements.description: Backdrop fade and popup scale animations require global CSS rules inside @layer priority1. StyleX cannot target data-attribute selectors, so these rules must live in the consumer's global stylesheet.

intents.reasoning: AlertDialog is the correct component when a destructive or irreversible action requires explicit user confirmation before proceeding.
intents.composition:
<AlertDialog.Root>
  <AlertDialog.Trigger render={<Button variant="outline" color="destructive">Delete</Button>} />
  <AlertDialog.Portal>
    <AlertDialog.Backdrop />
    <AlertDialog.Popup>
      <AlertDialog.Title>Delete this item?</AlertDialog.Title>
      <AlertDialog.Description>This action cannot be undone.</AlertDialog.Description>
      <AlertDialog.Actions>
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>} />
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button color="destructive">Delete</Button>} onClick={handleDelete} />
      </AlertDialog.Actions>
    </AlertDialog.Popup>
  </AlertDialog.Portal>
</AlertDialog.Root>

intents.reasoning: AlertDialog blocks interaction until the user explicitly acknowledges or confirms. Use when skipping or ignoring the prompt is not acceptable.
intents.composition:
<AlertDialog.Root>
  <AlertDialog.Trigger render={<Button>Continue</Button>} />
  <AlertDialog.Portal>
    <AlertDialog.Backdrop />
    <AlertDialog.Popup>
      <AlertDialog.Title>Accept terms?</AlertDialog.Title>
      <AlertDialog.Description>You must accept before continuing.</AlertDialog.Description>
      <AlertDialog.Actions>
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>} />
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button>Accept</Button>} onClick={handleAccept} />
      </AlertDialog.Actions>
    </AlertDialog.Popup>
  </AlertDialog.Portal>
</AlertDialog.Root>

intents.reasoning: AlertDialog prevents accidental data loss by forcing the user to confirm before discarding unsaved work.
intents.composition:
<AlertDialog.Root>
  <AlertDialog.Trigger render={<Button variant="ghost">Leave</Button>} />
  <AlertDialog.Portal>
    <AlertDialog.Backdrop />
    <AlertDialog.Popup>
      <AlertDialog.Title>Discard changes?</AlertDialog.Title>
      <AlertDialog.Description>You have unsaved changes that will be lost.</AlertDialog.Description>
      <AlertDialog.Actions>
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Keep editing</Button>} />
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button color="destructive">Discard</Button>} onClick={handleDiscard} />
      </AlertDialog.Actions>
    </AlertDialog.Popup>
  </AlertDialog.Portal>
</AlertDialog.Root>

avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Dialog instead. AlertDialog is for confirmations requiring acknowledgment, not general content display.
Use Toast for transient messages. AlertDialog blocks all interaction and is too disruptive for non-critical feedback.

avoidWhen.scenario: Simple yes/no questions that are easily reversible
avoidWhen.reasoning: If the action is easily undone, an AlertDialog adds unnecessary friction. Prefer an inline undo pattern or a Toast with an undo button.

avoidWhen.scenario: A dismissible overlay that can be closed by clicking outside
avoidWhen.reasoning: AlertDialog cannot be dismissed by clicking outside — that's by design. Use Dialog if you want backdrop dismissal.

examples.name: basic
examples.code:
<AlertDialog.Root>
  <AlertDialog.Trigger render={<Button variant="outline" color="destructive">Delete</Button>} />
  <AlertDialog.Portal>
    <AlertDialog.Backdrop />
    <AlertDialog.Popup>
      <AlertDialog.Title>Delete this item?</AlertDialog.Title>
      <AlertDialog.Description>This action cannot be undone.</AlertDialog.Description>
      <AlertDialog.Actions>
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>} />
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button color="destructive">Delete</Button>} />
      </AlertDialog.Actions>
    </AlertDialog.Popup>
  </AlertDialog.Portal>
</AlertDialog.Root>

examples.name: controlled
examples.code:
const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);

<AlertDialog.Root open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <AlertDialog.Trigger render={<Button color="destructive">Remove account</Button>} />
  <AlertDialog.Portal>
    <AlertDialog.Backdrop />
    <AlertDialog.Popup>
      <AlertDialog.Title>Remove your account?</AlertDialog.Title>
      <AlertDialog.Description>All data will be permanently deleted.</AlertDialog.Description>
      <AlertDialog.Actions>
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button>} />
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button color="destructive">Remove</Button>} onClick={handleRemove} />
      </AlertDialog.Actions>
    </AlertDialog.Popup>
  </AlertDialog.Portal>
</AlertDialog.Root>

examples.name: discard-changes
examples.code:
<AlertDialog.Root>
  <AlertDialog.Trigger render={<Button variant="ghost">Leave page</Button>} />
  <AlertDialog.Portal>
    <AlertDialog.Backdrop />
    <AlertDialog.Popup>
      <AlertDialog.Title>Discard changes?</AlertDialog.Title>
      <AlertDialog.Description>You have unsaved changes that will be lost.</AlertDialog.Description>
      <AlertDialog.Actions>
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button variant="ghost">Keep editing</Button>} />
        <AlertDialog.Close render={<Button color="destructive">Discard</Button>} />
      </AlertDialog.Actions>
    </AlertDialog.Popup>
  </AlertDialog.Portal>
</AlertDialog.Root>
